description:
I stopped in Miles City, Montana on the way back to Wyoming over the weekend and enjoyed a few hours with an old friend hunting moss agates on a gravel bar along the Yellowstone River.  Numerous killdeer skittered in front of us until taking flight with their signature "launch call" (my term  lol) and they left a plethora of tracks in the silty mud along with little raccoon handprints, deer and wild turkey tracks.  

This little one came as a surprise, though, for he was half hidden beneath the water and a rock initially, then jumped with a splash after being surprised.  Northern leopard frogs handle cold weather and high altitudes (up to 9800 feet) well, so I shouldn't be surprised by their range extending far to the north to the Great Slave Lake and Hudson Bay.  They're fairly common across the northern tier of the US, though they have lost range in western Montana (theories include agricultural chemicals and the introduction of trout in watersheds where they were not native).  I was also interested to learn that they can survive in the wild for up to nine years (12 years in captivity).  (9-11-2022)
My favorite fish
